title: Sentinel UI: Live Request Log Streaming via SSE with Backpressure and Filters
description: Implement a Server-Sent Events (SSE) endpoint that streams live pre-redacted request log events from the in-memory LogRingBuffer (TASK-051) to the UI. Support filtering by decision (permit/deny) and service_id. Implement backpressure handling: slow or disconnected clients must not block the logger or cause memory growth. Cap concurrent SSE connections. Test under load with multiple connections and rapid event ingestion.
objectives: Get events from LogRingBuffer subscriber queue (TASK-051)
Backpressure: if client is slow, drop events (not block) and send a 'events_dropped' SSE comment
Max 10 concurrent SSE connections — return 429 beyond limit
Clean up subscriber queue on client disconnect (avoid memory leak)

steps.title: Implement SSE streaming generator
Implement backpressure handling
steps.detail: LogRingBuffer.subscribe() returns a `asyncio.Queue(maxsize=200)`. When the sentinel pushes an event to subscribers (in `LogRingBuffer.append()`): use `queue.put_nowait(event)`. If `queue.full()`: call `queue.get_nowait()` to drop the OLDEST event and put the new one (ring behavior for subscriber queue too). Increment `events_dropped` counter. When dropping occurs, send an SSE comment to the client: `': events_dropped=1\n\n'` so the UI can show a warning indicator. This ensures the main logger thread is NEVER blocked by a slow UI client.

steps.title: Implement connection limit enforcement
steps.detail: Add `active_sse_connections: int = 0` to app state (thread-safe via `asyncio.Lock`). In the SSE route handler: acquire lock, check `active_sse_connections < 10`, increment, release lock. If limit reached: return `Response(status_code=429, content='Too Many Streaming Connections')`. On client disconnect (detected via `asyncio.CancelledError` or generator exhaustion): acquire lock, decrement `active_sse_connections`, call `LogRingBuffer.unsubscribe(queue)` to remove the queue from the subscriber list. This prevents unbounded memory growth from accumulated dead subscriber queues.

steps.title: Implement SSE FastAPI route
steps.detail: Route: `GET /ui/logs/stream` in `sentinel/ui/router.py`. Parameters: `filter_decision: str | None = Query(None, regex='^(permit|deny)$')`, `filter_service_id: str | None = Query(None, max_length=64)`. Handler creates subscriber queue, registers with ring buffer, creates StreamingResponse: `StreamingResponse(log_event_stream(queue, filter_decision, filter_service_id), media_type='text/event-stream', headers={'Cache-Control': 'no-cache', 'X-Accel-Buffering': 'no'})`. `X-Accel-Buffering: no` disables nginx proxy buffering for SSE. `Cache-Control: no-cache` prevents browser caching.

steps.title: UI integration for live log page
steps.detail: In `logs.html` template: add a 'Live Stream' button that opens `GET /ui/logs/stream` in an EventSource. Minimal JavaScript: `const evtSource = new EventSource('/ui/logs/stream?filter_decision=' + filter); evtSource.onmessage = e => { prependRowToTable(JSON.parse(e.data)); }`. Cap displayed rows at 500 (remove oldest from DOM on overflow). Show 'Events dropped' notice if SSE comment with `events_dropped` received. Add 'Stop Stream' button that calls `evtSource.close()`. Auto-reconnect: EventSource reconnects automatically if connection drops — this is the browser's built-in behavior.

steps.title: Tests
steps.detail: Test file: `tests/unit/ui/test_live_logs.py`. Cases: (1) SSE endpoint returns 200 text/event-stream content-type; (2) events pushed to ring buffer appear in stream within 100ms; (3) filter_decision=deny only streams deny events; (4) 11th concurrent connection returns 429; (5) slow client: push 300 events rapidly, verify queue doesn't exceed maxsize=200 (events dropped); (6) client disconnect decrements active_sse_connections to 0; (7) keep-alive comment sent every 15s timeout. Use `httpx.AsyncClient` with `stream()` context for SSE response testing.

security_constraints: SSE endpoint is UI-only (localhost-bound per TASK-054) — not accessible from external network
No auth required on SSE endpoint since it's localhost-only (same as other /ui/* routes)
filter_service_id is validated with max_length and no special chars to prevent injection via query param
Events are pre-redacted by SentinelLogger — SSE endpoint performs no additional redaction (avoids double-processing)
Connection limit of 10 prevents local DoS via SSE connection exhaustion
acceptance_criteria: SSE stream delivers events in real-time (< 100ms from Logger.log_request to client receipt)
Events dropped when client queue is full — logger never blocked
Client disconnect properly releases subscriber queue (no leak)
Filter by decision correctly applied server-side
